title: Playwright E2E: protected route redirects unauthenticated users to /login
body:
## Issue description

The `<ProtectedRoute>` component (`client/src/components/ProtectedRoute/ProtectedRoute.tsx`) redirects unauthenticated users to `/login` using React Router's `<Navigate to="/login" replace />`. This is a critical security and UX behavior: every authenticated page in the app must be inaccessible without a valid MSAL session.

Unit tests for this behavior exist (`client/src/__tests__/components/ProtectedRoute.test.tsx`) using MemoryRouter + mocked `useAuth`. However, there is no browser-level E2E test that verifies the redirect actually fires in a real browser against the deployed application — where MSAL initialisation, the SPA shell, and nginx's `try_files` fallback all interact.

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### Test scenarios to implement

All specs live in `client/e2e/protected-route.spec.ts` and use the `unauthenticatedContext()` helper (fresh browser context, no stored auth state).

**Scenario 1 — Direct navigation to `/groups` redirects to `/login`**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates directly to `https://<base-url>/groups`
- Then: the browser ends up at `<base-url>/login`
- And: the Login page heading / sign-in button is visible

**Scenario 2 — Direct navigation to a group detail page redirects to `/login`**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates to `https://<base-url>/groups/some-group-id`
- Then: the browser ends up at `<base-url>/login`

**Scenario 3 — Direct navigation to an event detail page redirects to `/login`**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates to `https://<base-url>/groups/some-group-id/events/some-event-id`
- Then: the browser ends up at `<base-url>/login`

**Scenario 4 — Direct navigation to `/groups/:groupId/events/new` redirects to `/login`**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates to `https://<base-url>/groups/some-group-id/events/new`
- Then: the browser ends up at `<base-url>/login`

**Scenario 5 — Direct navigation to `/profile` redirects to `/login`**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates to `https://<base-url>/profile`
- Then: the browser ends up at `<base-url>/login`

**Scenario 6 — `/login` is accessible without auth (no redirect loop)**
- Given: a browser with no MSAL auth state
- When: the user navigates to `https://<base-url>/login`
- Then: the page renders without redirect
- And: the sign-in button and brand header are visible
- And: the URL remains `/login`

### Assertions style
- Use `expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/login/)` rather than string-equal comparisons to tolerate trailing slashes or query params added by MSAL.
- Use `page.getByRole` or `page.getByText` locators anchored to semantic elements (button, heading) rather than CSS selectors or test-id attributes.
- Each test must have a maximum wait of 10 s for the URL assertion; use `{ timeout: 10000 }` explicitly.

## Additional notes

- `ProtectedRoute` renders a "Loading…" state while MSAL resolves (`isLoading === true`). Playwright must wait past this transient state; the `toHaveURL` assertion already does this implicitly.
- The Login page (`client/src/pages/Login/Login.tsx`) redirects to `/groups` if the user *is* authenticated — so tests using `unauthenticatedContext()` must not inadvertently carry over any MSAL cache from other test files. Use Playwright's `--workers 1` or isolate contexts per test.
- The nginx container serves a SPA fallback (`try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html`), so deep links load `index.html` and React Router handles the redirect client-side; E2E tests confirm this full chain works end-to-end.
- The `LoginPage` Page Object (introduced in the infrastructure issue) should provide `expectVisible()` and `url()` helpers used by these specs.
- These tests require **no authenticated session** and therefore do not depend on `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_USER_EMAIL`/`PASSWORD` secrets; they can run in CI on every PR without Azure AD credentials.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `client/e2e/protected-route.spec.ts` exists with all six scenarios implemented.
- [ ] Each scenario uses a fresh unauthenticated browser context (no shared auth state).
- [ ] All six tests pass against the deployed (or locally served) application.
- [ ] Tests use `page.getByRole` / `page.getByText` locators, not brittle CSS selectors.
- [ ] `expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/login/)` is used for redirect assertions.
- [ ] Tests do not require Azure AD test-user credentials (no authenticated state needed).
- [ ] All six tests are included in and pass the `npm run test:e2e` run.
- [ ] Test results appear in the CI artifact (JUnit XML + HTML report).
